questions.question: In a chemical reaction diagram, A and B react to form C and D. What are A and B called?
State how C and D differ from A and B.
questions.answer: C and D are products (new substances formed); A and B are reactants (starting substances).

questions.question: State why A and B on reacting result in formation of C and D.
questions.answer: During a chemical reaction, atoms of the reactants rearrange to form new substances (products) with different properties.

questions.question: Write the word equation for the reaction of magnesium with dilute sulphuric acid.
questions.answer: Magnesium + Dilute sulphuric acid → Magnesium sulphate + Hydrogen

questions.question: Write the molecular equation for the reaction of magnesium with dilute sulphuric acid.
Write the word equation for: CuCO₃ → CuO + CO₂
questions.answer: Copper carbonate → Copper oxide + Carbon dioxide

questions.question: Write the word equation for: ZnCO₃ → ZnO + CO₂
questions.answer: Zinc carbonate → Zinc oxide + Carbon dioxide

questions.question: Write the word equation for: 2HgO → 2Hg + O₂
Write the word equation for: 2PbO₂ → 2PbO + O₂
Write the word equation for: CuSO₄ + Fe → FeSO₄ + Cu
questions.answer: Copper(II) sulphate + Iron → Iron(II) sulphate + Copper

questions.question: Give a word equation for a chemical reaction resulting in the evolution of hydrogen gas.
questions.answer: Zinc + Dilute sulphuric acid → Zinc sulphate + Hydrogen↑

questions.question: Give a word equation for a chemical reaction resulting in the evolution of oxygen gas.
questions.answer: Potassium chlorate (heat, catalyst MnO₂) → Potassium chloride + Oxygen↑

questions.question: Give a word equation for a chemical reaction resulting in the evolution of carbon dioxide gas.
questions.answer: Calcium carbonate + Dilute hydrochloric acid → Calcium chloride + Water + Carbon dioxide↑

questions.question: Give a word equation for a chemical reaction resulting in the evolution of hydrogen chloride gas.
questions.answer: Sodium chloride + Concentrated sulphuric acid → Sodium bisulphate + Hydrogen chloride↑

questions.question: Give a word equation for a chemical reaction resulting in the evolution of sulphur dioxide gas.
questions.answer: Sodium sulphite + Dilute sulphuric acid → Sodium sulphate + Water + Sulphur dioxide↑

questions.question: Give an equation for evolution of gas by thermal decomposition of a metallic carbonate.
questions.answer: Zinc carbonate →(heat) Zinc oxide + Carbon dioxide↑

questions.question: Give an equation for evolution of gas by thermal decomposition of a metallic oxide.
Write the word equation for: AgNO₃ + HCl → HNO₃ + AgCl↓
questions.answer: Silver nitrate + Hydrochloric acid → Nitric acid + Silver chloride↓

questions.question: Name the precipitate formed in the reaction of silver nitrate with hydrochloric acid.
State the colour of the precipitate silver chloride.
Write the word equation for: FeSO₄ + 2NaOH → Na₂SO₄ + Fe(OH)₂↓
questions.answer: Iron(II) sulphate + Sodium hydroxide → Sodium sulphate + Iron(II) hydroxide↓

questions.question: Name the precipitate formed in reaction of iron(II) sulphate with sodium hydroxide.
State the colour of iron(II) hydroxide precipitate.
Write the word equation for: CuSO₄ + 2NaOH → Na₂SO₄ + Cu(OH)₂↓
questions.answer: Copper(II) sulphate + Sodium hydroxide → Sodium sulphate + Copper(II) hydroxide↓

questions.question: Name the precipitate formed in reaction of copper(II) sulphate with sodium hydroxide.
State the colour of copper(II) hydroxide precipitate.
Write the word equation for: Pb(NO₃)₂ + 2NH₄OH → 2NH₄NO₃ + Pb(OH)₂↓
questions.answer: Lead nitrate + Ammonium hydroxide → Ammonium nitrate + Lead hydroxide↓

questions.question: Name the precipitate formed in reaction of lead nitrate with ammonium hydroxide.
State the colour of lead hydroxide precipitate.
Give a word equation for an exothermic reaction between a solid and a neutral liquid (CaO + H₂O).
questions.answer: Calcium oxide + Water → Calcium hydroxide + Heat

questions.question: Give a word equation for an exothermic reaction between an acidic gas and a neutral gas (2SO₂ + O₂).
questions.answer: Sulphur dioxide + Oxygen → Sulphur trioxide + Heat

questions.question: Give a word equation for an exothermic reaction between neutral gases including nitrogen (N₂ + 3H₂).
Give a word equation for an exothermic reaction between neutral gases including oxygen (2H₂ + O₂).
Why is rusting an exothermic reaction but the heat evolved is unnoticeable?
questions.answer: Rusting is a very slow process, so the heat is released very gradually over a long time and is not noticeable.

questions.question: Why are concrete slabs set slowly during building structures?
questions.answer: To avoid cracking due to heat released during the setting (exothermic) process. Slow setting allows gradual heat dissipation.

questions.question: Why are certain exothermic reactions in the human body beneficial?
questions.answer: They provide the required energy for body functions and help maintain body temperature.

questions.question: Why are solid reactants powdered and mixed before reacting?
questions.answer: To increase the surface area of contact between the reactants, which makes the reaction faster.
questions.source_section: C. Conditions Necessary for Chemical Reaction

questions.question: Name a non-metal that reacts explosively with iodine on close contact.
questions.source_section: C. Conditions Necessary for Chemical Reaction

questions.question: Name a metal that reacts explosively with cold water.
questions.source_section: C. Conditions Necessary for Chemical Reaction

questions.question: Name a reaction that takes place in solution state only.
questions.source_section: C. Conditions Necessary for Chemical Reaction

questions.question: Name a reaction that takes place on heating the reactant(s).
questions.source_section: C. Conditions Necessary for Chemical Reaction

questions.question: Name a reaction that takes place on supplying light energy.
questions.source_section: C. Conditions Necessary for Chemical Reaction

questions.question: Name a reaction in which electricity dissociates the reactant.
questions.source_section: C. Conditions Necessary for Chemical Reaction

questions.question: Name a reaction that takes place on subjecting reactants to pressure.
questions.source_section: C. Conditions Necessary for Chemical Reaction

questions.question: Name a reaction that takes place in the presence of a catalyst.
questions.answer: Decomposition of potassium chlorate in the presence of MnO₂ catalyst to give potassium chloride and oxygen.
questions.source_section: C. Conditions Necessary for Chemical Reaction

questions.question: Why is silver bromide stored in a brown thick glass bottle?
questions.answer: Silver bromide decomposes when exposed to light, so it is stored in dark (brown) bottles to prevent this decomposition.
questions.source_section: C. Conditions Necessary for Chemical Reaction

questions.question: Why are solid reactants not generally subjected to pressure changes?
questions.answer: Solids are almost incompressible, so changes in pressure have very little effect on reactions involving solid reactants.
questions.source_section: C. Conditions Necessary for Chemical Reaction

questions.question: Why is dilute sulphuric acid added to water before electrolysis?
questions.answer: To increase the conductivity of water, as pure water is a poor conductor of electricity.
questions.source_section: C. Conditions Necessary for Chemical Reaction

questions.question: Why do most chemical reactions require heating?
questions.answer: To provide activation energy — the minimum energy required for the reactants to start reacting and form products.
questions.source_section: C. Conditions Necessary for Chemical Reaction

questions.question: How does a positive catalyst differ from a negative catalyst?
questions.answer: A positive catalyst increases the rate of a chemical reaction, while a negative catalyst (inhibitor) decreases the rate of a chemical reaction.
questions.source_section: C. Conditions Necessary for Chemical Reaction

questions.question: Why is a promoter used along with the catalyst in the manufacture of ammonia?
questions.answer: A promoter increases the efficiency of the catalyst, making the reaction proceed faster and more completely.
questions.source_section: C. Conditions Necessary for Chemical Reaction

questions.question: Complete the revision table of symbols and valencies for common elements and radicals.
questions.answer: Monovalent (+): K⁺, Na⁺, Cu⁺, Hg⁺, Ag⁺, NH₄⁺, H⁺ | Divalent (+): Ca²⁺, Mg²⁺, Zn²⁺, Cu²⁺, Hg²⁺, Fe²⁺, Pb²⁺ | Trivalent (+): Al³⁺, Fe³⁺ | Tetravalent (+): Pb⁴⁺ | Monovalent (-): Cl⁻, Br⁻, I⁻, OH⁻, HCO₃⁻, HSO₃⁻, HSO₄⁻, NO₃⁻ | Divalent (-): CO₃²⁻, SO₃²⁻, SO₄²⁻, S²⁻, O²⁻ | Trivalent (-): PO₄³⁻

Balance the equation: N₂ + H₂ → NH₃
Balance the equation: H₂ + Cl₂ → HCl
Write the word equation for: NH₃ + HCl → NH₄Cl
Write the word equation for: Fe + 2HCl → FeCl₂ + H₂
questions.answer: Iron + Hydrochloric acid → Iron(II) chloride + Hydrogen

questions.question: Write the word equation for: AgNO₃ + HCl → AgCl + HNO₃
questions.answer: Silver nitrate + Hydrochloric acid → Silver chloride + Nitric acid

questions.question: Write the word equation for: NaOH + HNO₃ → NaNO₃ + H₂O
questions.answer: Sodium hydroxide + Nitric acid → Sodium nitrate + Water

questions.question: Write the word equation for: CaCO₃ + 2HNO₃ → Ca(NO₃)₂ + H₂O + CO₂
questions.answer: Calcium carbonate + Nitric acid → Calcium nitrate + Water + Carbon dioxide

questions.question: Write the word equation for: FeS + H₂SO₄ → FeSO₄ + H₂S
questions.answer: Iron sulphide + Sulphuric acid → Iron sulphate + Hydrogen sulphide

questions.question: Write the word equation for: 2NaOH + H₂SO₄ → Na₂SO₄ + 2H₂O
questions.answer: Sodium hydroxide + Sulphuric acid → Sodium sulphate + Water

questions.question: Write the word equation for: FeCl₃ + 3NaOH → 3NaCl + Fe(OH)₃
questions.answer: Iron(III) chloride + Sodium hydroxide → Sodium chloride + Iron(III) hydroxide

questions.question: Write the word equation for: CuO + H₂SO₄ → CuSO₄ + H₂O
questions.answer: Copper(II) oxide + Sulphuric acid → Copper(II) sulphate + Water

questions.question: Write the word equation for: CaCl₂ + Na₂SO₃ → 2NaCl + CaSO₃
questions.answer: Calcium chloride + Sodium sulphite → Sodium chloride + Calcium sulphite

questions.question: Write the word equation for: NH₄OH + HCl → NH₄Cl + H₂O
questions.answer: Ammonium hydroxide + Hydrochloric acid → Ammonium chloride + Water

questions.question: Write the word equation for: PbO + 2HNO₃ → Pb(NO₃)₂ + H₂O
questions.answer: Lead(II) oxide + Nitric acid → Lead(II) nitrate + Water
